sections.title: Workflow 2 — Trade Business Development
sections.description: Outreach and follow-up with trade prospects (dealerships, garages, body shops, fleet operators).

tasks.title: Workflow framework drafted
tasks.notes: workflows/workflow-02-trade-development.md

tasks.title: Trade Development Agent defined
tasks.notes: agents/02-trade-development-agent.md — created 2026-03-15 evening. Pending Jack review + template approval before activation.

tasks.title: Define: what counts as a trade lead?
tasks.notes: Any automotive business with a fleet — dealerships, garages, body shops, tyre bays, fleet operators, insurance/repair networks, funeral businesses, any business with vehicles.

tasks.title: Define: outreach cadence (timing + touches)
tasks.notes: Day 1 → Day 4 → Day 10 → stop. Confirmed.

tasks.title: Define: auto-send vs human review threshold
tasks.notes: Marcus can auto-send. Messaging must be right first — outreach templates to be drafted and approved before activation.

tasks.title: Define: what outcome = qualified lead?
tasks.notes: Core ask = trial job (for outreach purposes). To be refined per branch.

tasks.title: Apollo.io API connected
tasks.notes: API key received. Scaffold at integrations/apollo-trade-prospecting.md. Awaiting plan tier + prospect profile.

tasks.title: CRM sync vs in-flight Apollo use decision
tasks.notes: CRM handles routing — leads arrive pre-allocated to the franchisee. Marcus works on behalf of individual franchisees doing outbound. No routing logic needed in Marcus.

tasks.title: Draft outbound trade outreach messaging templates
tasks.notes: Approved by Jack 2026-03-15. Ready to activate once rate cards + postcode map received (Monday).

tasks.title: Inbound trade enquiry workflow
tasks.notes: Documented in workflows/workflow-02-inbound-trade-enquiry.md. Covers identification, triage, response, qualification, national account escalation. Trade lead log scaffolded at trade/trade-lead-log.md. Ready for activation once rate cards received.

tasks.notes: National account = business requiring service across more than one TWS branch (insurers, fleet, dealer groups).

tasks.title: Define: who has final allocation authority?

tasks.title: Define fairness principles
tasks.notes: No minimum volume — purely whether multiple branches are involved.

sections.title: Email Integration — Clook IMAP/SMTP
sections.description: Per-branch Clook-hosted mailboxes via IMAP/SMTP (not Outlook/Azure). Phased rollout: dummy pilot first, then all 23 branches.

tasks.title: Azure app registration requested from IT
tasks.notes: Emailed 2026-03-15. Requested: Tenant ID, Client ID, Client Secret, admin consent for Mail.ReadWrite + Mail.Send + Calendars.ReadWrite

tasks.title: Receive IMAP/SMTP credentials from IT (per mailbox)
tasks.notes: IT confirmed mailboxes are on Clook (not Microsoft 365). Need: IMAP server + port, SMTP server + port, username + password per mailbox. Azure app registration not required.

tasks.title: Set up dummy forwarding mailbox for pilot
tasks.notes: Branch emails forward to central dummy address for safe testing

tasks.title: Install Himalaya email CLI (IMAP/SMTP)
tasks.notes: Himalaya v1.2.0 installed at ~/bin/himalaya. Config template created at integrations/email-setup.md. Ready to connect once IT provides IMAP/SMTP credentials. (Note: clawhub doesn't exist — himalaya is the bundled OpenClaw skill for IMAP/SMTP.)

tasks.title: Connect pilot mailbox and test triage logic
tasks.notes: Phase 1: read + categorise + flag to Jack only. No auto-sending.

tasks.title: Enable auto-responses for standard enquiries
tasks.notes: Phase 2: only after triage accuracy verified.

tasks.title: Roll out to all 23 branch mailboxes
tasks.notes: Phase 3: replicate once pilot confirmed solid.

tasks.title: IMAP credentials — pilot mailbox
tasks.notes: Need from IT: IMAP host, port, username, password for the pilot/dummy forwarding mailbox.

tasks.title: SMTP credentials — outbound sending
tasks.notes: IT noted they use a separate SMTP server for outgoing mail. Need: SMTP host, port, username, password.

sections.title: System Infrastructure (K1–K8)
sections.description: Health monitoring, cost tracking, security, version control, and backup infrastructure. Added 2026-03-19.

tasks.title: K1 — Health Monitoring Heartbeat System
tasks.notes: Daily automated checks: gateway health, WhatsApp channel, model API, data integrity, open quote follow-ups, disk space, API key expiry. Summary to Discord #marcus-setup at 08:00 GMT. Needs heartbeat cron configured. Jack to confirm check list before build.

tasks.title: K2 — Model Usage and Cost Tracking
tasks.notes: Log every AI API call — model, task type, tokens (in/out/cache), estimated cost, session ID, channel. Daily/weekly/monthly reports. JSONL at data/usage-logs/YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl. Needs confirmation of what token data is accessible via session_status.

tasks.title: K3 — AI Writing Humanizer
tasks.notes: Post-process outbound copy to remove AI patterns, over-formal language, excessive hedging. Applies to customer WhatsApp, trade outreach, complaints, follow-ups. Much achievable via SOUL.md tone rules. Full humanizer agent is a separate build — needs spec.

tasks.title: K4 — Git Auto-Sync
tasks.notes: Auto-commit and push workspace changes every 6 hours + on significant events (SOUL.md update, pricing update, new agent definition). Depends on marcus-core repo (K8) being created first. Straightforward cron job once remote is set.

tasks.title: K5 — Database Backup
tasks.notes: Done 2026-03-19 evening. scripts/backup.sh created — rsync all critical dirs + files to ~/.openclaw/backups/YYYY-MM-DD/. First backup ran successfully (19 items). Cron scheduled at 02:00 GMT daily. 30-day retention enforced. Weekly/monthly offsite deferred until marcus-core repo (K8) is created.

tasks.title: K6 — Security and Safety Layers
tasks.notes: Rules-based items (prompt injection defence, data protection, approval gates) implemented in SOUL.md + AGENTS.md. Automated checks (nightly credential scan, gateway exposure check, MEMORY.md scan, repo size monitor) to be added to K1 heartbeat.

tasks.title: K7 — Security Council Nightly Codebase Review
tasks.notes: Nightly 3:30am review from 4 perspectives: offensive, defensive, data privacy, operational realism. Numbered findings (Critical/High/Medium/Low). Critical = immediate Discord alert. Interactive: Jack can reply 'expand [N]' for details. Non-trivial build — needs cron + isolated subagent. Scoping required before implementation.

tasks.title: K8 — marcus-core Private Git Repository
tasks.notes: Jack to create JackBmurphy/marcus-core (private) on GitHub. Add PAT with push access. Set as git remote for workspace. Enables K4 auto-sync, version control of the Marcus system, and future forking for multi-branch / multi-business deployments. Everything else is ready to go.
